tests.name: User switches from 1-minute to 15-minute window to see resource trend wider window shows more historical data at lower resolution
Time window presets have correct resolution and capacity 1-minute window: 600 samples at 100ms interval
Time window presets have correct resolution and capacity 5-minute window: 600 samples at 500ms interval
Time window presets have correct resolution and capacity 15-minute window: 900 samples at 1000ms interval
Session-length window computes dynamic resolution for full history 42-minute session targets 300-900 data points
Session-length window computes dynamic resolution for full history 5-minute session also targets 300-900 data points
Stats computed from 5-minute window differ from 1-minute window spike outside 1m range but within 5m range produces different peak stats
Time window selection is independent of view mode time window is a separate state dimension from aggregate/detail mode
1-minute window buffer at capacity wraps correctly 601st sample evicts the oldest, preserving 600-sample window
Partially filled window still produces valid stats stats computed correctly when buffer has fewer samples than capacity
Time window persists when switching from aggregate to session detail selected time window survives aggregate-to-detail-to-aggregate navigation
Charts update when time window selection changes changing time window selection updates the displayed window label
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions renders the menu bar with View entry
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions renders the status bar footer
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions renders session rows when sessions exist
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions renders a <main> element as the top-level container
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions shows loading state before data arrives
App smoke test — catches black screen regressions shows error state when backend fails
Layout structure smoke tests renders the sidebar with plugin icons
Layout structure smoke tests renders the zone container for layout engine
Layout structure smoke tests renders the main zone with session content
Layout structure smoke tests renders sidebar, zone container, and status bar in correct order
Layout structure smoke tests status bar stays anchored at the bottom (flex-shrink: 0 in CSS)
Layout structure smoke tests session list renders inside the main zone without flicker
Notification plugin smoke tests renders a sidebar icon for the Notifications view
Notification plugin smoke tests clicking the Notifications sidebar icon shows the Notification Center view
Notification plugin smoke tests Notification Center shows empty state when no notifications
Notification plugin smoke tests Notification Center has a DND toggle button
Raj switches time window and sees data from a different resolution buffer 1m and 5m windows contain different numbers of samples for the same input stream
1-minute window provides high-resolution data at 100ms intervals 1m buffer accepts samples at 100ms interval
5-minute window downsamples to 500ms intervals 5m buffer captures fewer samples from high-frequency input
15-minute window downsamples to 1000ms intervals 15m buffer captures at 1-second intervals
Returning to 1m preserves high-resolution data 1m buffer retains full resolution while 5m view was active
Each window buffer produces valid chart points all three preset windows produce non-empty chart coordinates
Session shorter than selected window shows available data only 3-minute session viewed in 15m window shows 3 minutes of data
Window with no accumulated data returns empty samples newly created buffer returns empty for all windows
Requesting an unknown window ID returns empty samples invalid window ID returns empty array gracefully
Session window dynamically adjusts sample interval based on duration 45-minute session produces reasonable sample interval and capacity
Session window dynamically adjusts sample interval based on duration short 2-minute session still produces a valid configuration
All four windows accumulate data independently and concurrently a single append call feeds all three preset windows based on interval
Store provides window-specific buffers via multiSessionStore wiring getAggregateWindowBuffer returns different data for 1m vs 5m
Store provides window-specific buffers via multiSessionStore wiring getSessionWindowBuffer returns per-session data for selected window
Store provides window-specific buffers via multiSessionStore wiring appendSessionSample feeds all three window buffers per category
Store provides window-specific buffers via multiSessionStore wiring existing getAggregateBuffer and getSessionBuffer continue to work
@property: 1m window always has at least as many samples as 5m for same input 1m sample count >= 5m sample count for any input sequence
@property: window buffer capacity is never exceeded no window exceeds its configured capacity regardless of input volume
App renders via plugin system and layout engine renders the ZoneRenderer container instead of hardcoded views
App renders via plugin system and layout engine renders session list inside the main zone via view registry
App renders via plugin system and layout engine preserves the MenuBar with View entry
App renders via plugin system and layout engine preserves the status bar footer with live metrics
App.tsx delegates view rendering to Layout Engine does not render SessionListView or EventDetailView directly in JSX
User sees per-session metric graphs update as events arrive appending a sample to a session creates per-category time-series data
Each session maintains independent buffers per category two sessions have separate token buffers with different values
Removing a session cleans up its per-category buffers removed session's buffers are no longer accessible
Appending to a nonexistent session is a no-op no error when appending to unknown session ID
Aggregate token buffer sums across all sessions aggregate token value equals sum of per-session token rates
Aggregate cost buffer sums across all sessions aggregate cost value equals sum of per-session cost rates
Context category has no aggregate buffer aggregate context buffer is empty or not populated
Subscribers are notified when samples are appended callback invoked after appendSessionSample
Unsubscribed callbacks are not invoked callback not called after unsubscribe
Existing store operations unchanged after extension addSession, removeSession, updateSession, getSessions, getSession still work
Getting buffer for unknown category returns empty buffer unknown category ID returns empty or undefined buffer
Adding same session twice is idempotent duplicate addSession does not reset existing buffers
Aggregate buffer updates when a session is removed aggregate recalculates after session removal
Multiple rapid samples accumulate in ring buffer consecutive appends increase buffer sample count
@property: context aggregate buffer is never populated regardless of session count for any number of sessions, context aggregate remains empty
@property: aggregate sum always equals sum of per-session latest values tokens aggregate latest value equals sum of per-session latest values
Hook processor feeds per-category samples after metrics update Token rate sample derived from instantaneous rate on event
Hook processor feeds per-category samples after metrics update Agent count sample derived from session metrics
Hook processor feeds per-category samples after metrics update Existing single-session pipeline unchanged after extension
Raj sees a visible chart line when sessions are generating data aggregate chart has renderable points when 3 sessions are active
Aggregate chart reflects combined token rate across sessions aggregate buffer latest value equals sum of per-session rates
Per-session mini charts show individual session data each session buffer produces sparkline points with non-zero amplitude
Per-session mini charts show individual session data higher-rate session produces higher amplitude sparkline than lower-rate session
Chart line extends rightward as new samples arrive appending samples increases the number of chart points
Chart continues after a session ends removing a session leaves remaining sessions contributing to aggregate
Chart continues after a session ends removed session no longer appears in per-session buffer lookups
Empty state when no sessions are active aggregate buffer has no samples when no sessions exist
Empty state when no sessions are active chart point preparation returns empty array for zero samples
Chart handles maximum buffer size without data corruption buffer at capacity evicts oldest while maintaining renderable points
Chart renders correctly with single data point one sample produces one chart point at a valid position
All zero token rates produce flat baseline, not blank chart zero-rate samples create points at the bottom of the chart
Appending data to a session that does not exist is safe no crash or data corruption when session ID is unknown
@property: aggregate buffer is non-empty whenever active sessions have events for any combination of 1-5 sessions with events, aggregate has samples
User sees Usage tab after norbert-usage loads plugin registers views, tab, status item, and hook processor on load
Gauge Cluster view supports floating panel mode registers with floatMetric for session cost pill display
Usage Dashboard is the primary view usage-dashboard registers as primaryView for default display
Plugin operates with degraded functionality when API unavailable continues with views and tab when status item registration fails
Plugin uses only public NorbertPlugin API norbert-usage imports no internal Norbert modules

multiSessionStore add and remove addSession with duplicate ID is idempotent
multiSessionStore add and remove removeSession removes tracked session

multiSessionStore update and query getSession returns undefined for unknown session
multiSessionStore update and query tracks add and remove of sessions independently

multiSessionStore aggregate window buffer does not aggregate non-aggregatable categories (context)

multiSessionStore buffer fallback paths getAggregateWindowBuffer returns empty buffer for category with no samples

computeEffectiveYMax returns 1 when samples are empty and yMax is undefined
computeEffectiveYMax adds 10% headroom above peak when peak is positive
computeEffectiveYMax falls back to yMax when all values are zero
computeEffectiveYMax falls back to 1 when all values are zero and yMax is undefined
computeEffectiveYMax uses costRate field when specified

hexToRgba handles uppercase hex
hexToRgba returns white fallback for invalid hex
hexToRgba returns white fallback for empty string

computeTooltipLeft flips tooltip to the left when it would overflow right edge
computeTooltipLeft flips at exact boundary
